- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Build a distributable .app bundle for the menubar app.
# Output: dist/AI Usages Tracker.app (ad-hoc signed, unsealed).
#
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
PACKAGE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/AIUsagesTrackers"
DIST_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/dist"

APP_DISPLAY_NAME="AI Usages Tracker"
APP_BINARY_NAME="AIUsagesTrackers"
BUNDLE_ID="io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker"
BUNDLE_VERSION="${BUNDLE_VERSION:-0.1.0}"
MIN_MACOS_VERSION="14.0"

APP_BUNDLE="$DIST_DIR/$APP_DISPLAY_NAME.app"
CONTENTS="$APP_BUNDLE/Contents"
MACOS_DIR="$CONTENTS/MacOS"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS/Resources"
BUILD_CONFIG="release"
BUILD_DIR="$PACKAGE_DIR/.build/$BUILD_CONFIG"

echo "→ Building release binaries"
cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product "$APP_BINARY_NAME"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product IconExporter

# Resolve absolute build paths (swift emits a symlink in some configurations).
APP_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
ICON_EXPORTER_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/IconExporter"

echo "→ Assembling bundle at $APP_BUNDLE"
rm -rf "$APP_BUNDLE"
mkdir -p "$MACOS_DIR" "$RESOURCES_DIR"

echo "→ Rendering .icns from AppIconView"
ICONSET_TMP="$(mktemp -d)/AppIcon.iconset"
mkdir -p "$ICONSET_TMP"
"$ICON_EXPORTER_BIN" "$ICONSET_TMP"
iconutil -c icns "$ICONSET_TMP" -o "$RESOURCES_DIR/AppIcon.icns"

echo "→ Copying executable"
cp "$APP_BIN" "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
chmod +x "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"

echo "→ Writing Info.plist"
cat > "$CONTENTS/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
  <string>$APP_BINARY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>APPL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>
  <string>AppIcon</string>
  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
  <string>$MIN_MACOS_VERSION</string>
  <key>LSUIElement</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHumanReadableCopyright</key>
  <string>© $(date +%Y) Frédéric Camblor</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST

echo "→ Ad-hoc code-signing (required for Gatekeeper on unsigned binaries)"
codesign --force --deep --sign - "$APP_BUNDLE"

echo
echo "✓ Bundle ready: $APP_BUNDLE"
echo "  Launch with: open \"$APP_BUNDLE\""

4	
5	public enum MenuBarSegmentIssue: Equatable, Sendable {
6	    /// `.currentlyActive` was selected but no account for the vendor is flagged active.
7	    case noActiveAccount(vendor: Vendor)
8	    /// A specific account was selected but it's absent from the usages file.
9	    case accountNotFound(vendor: Vendor, email: AccountEmail)
10	    /// The target metric name is not present on the resolved account.
11	    case metricNotFound(metricName: String)
12	    /// The metric exists but its kind doesn't match the configured display variant
13	    /// (e.g. timeWindow display on a pay-as-you-go metric). Happens when the user
14	    /// changes the underlying metric kind externally.
15	    case metricKindMismatch
16	}
17	
18	public struct ResolvedMenuBarSegment: Sendable {
19	    public let config: MenuBarSegmentConfig
20	    public let rendered: MenuBarSegment?
21	    public let issue: MenuBarSegmentIssue?
22	
23	    public init(config: MenuBarSegmentConfig, rendered: MenuBarSegment?, issue: MenuBarSegmentIssue?) {
24	        self.config = config
25	        self.rendered = rendered
26	        self.issue = issue
27	    }
28	}
29	
30	// MARK: - Resolver
31	
32	/// Converts a `MenuBarSegmentConfig` + a snapshot of usage entries into either a
33	/// renderable `MenuBarSegment` or a structured issue that the Settings UI can
34	/// surface to the user.
35	public enum MenuBarSegmentResolver {
36	
37	    public static func resolve(
38	        config: MenuBarSegmentConfig,
39	        entries: [VendorUsageEntry],
40	        now: Date
41	    ) -> ResolvedMenuBarSegment {
42	        let vendorEntries = entries.filter { $0.vendor == config.vendor }
43	
44	        let entry: VendorUsageEntry?
45	        let resolveIssue: MenuBarSegmentIssue?
46	        switch config.account {
47	        case .currentlyActive:
48	            if let active = vendorEntries.first(where: { $0.isActive }) {
49	                entry = active
50	                resolveIssue = nil
51	            } else if vendorEntries.count == 1 {
52	                // No account is flagged active, but there is only one — treat it as implicitly active
53	                // so the user sees their data without having to wait for the monitor to sync.
54	                entry = vendorEntries[0]
55	                resolveIssue = nil
56	            } else {
57	                entry = nil
58	                resolveIssue = .noActiveAccount(vendor: config.vendor)
59	            }
60	        case .specific(let email):
61	            if let match = vendorEntries.first(where: { $0.account == email }) {
62	                entry = match
63	                resolveIssue = nil
64	            } else {
65	                entry = nil
66	                resolveIssue = .accountNotFound(vendor: config.vendor, email: email)
67	            }
68	        }
69	
70	        guard let entry else {
71	            return ResolvedMenuBarSegment(config: config, rendered: nil, issue: resolveIssue)
72	        }
73	
74	        guard let metric = entry.metrics.first(where: { metricName($0) == config.metricName }) else {
75	            return ResolvedMenuBarSegment(
76	                config: config,
77	                rendered: nil,
78	                issue: .metricNotFound(metricName: config.metricName)
79	            )
80	        }
81	
82	        switch (metric, config.display) {
83	        case let (.timeWindow(_, resetAt, windowDuration, usagePercent), .timeWindow(display)):
84	            let segment = renderTimeWindow(
85	                display: display,
86	                vendor: config.vendor,
87	                resetAt: resetAt,
88	                windowDuration: windowDuration,
89	                usagePercent: usagePercent,
90	                now: now
91	            )
92	            return ResolvedMenuBarSegment(config: config, rendered: segment, issue: nil)
93	
94	        case let (.payAsYouGo(_, currentAmount, currency), .payAsYouGo):
95	            let segment = renderPayAsYouGo(currentAmount: currentAmount, currency: currency)
96	            return ResolvedMenuBarSegment(config: config, rendered: segment, issue: nil)
97	
98	        default:
99	            return ResolvedMenuBarSegment(config: config, rendered: nil, issue: .metricKindMismatch)
100	        }
101	    }
102	
103	    private static func metricName(_ metric: UsageMetric) -> String? {
104	        switch metric {
105	        case .timeWindow(let name, _, _, _):   return name
106	        case .payAsYouGo(let name, _, _):      return name
107	        case .unknown:                         return nil
108	        }
109	    }
110	
111	    // MARK: Rendering
112	
113	    private static func renderTimeWindow(
114	        display: TimeWindowDisplay,
115	        vendor: Vendor,
116	        resetAt: ISODate?,
117	        windowDuration: DurationMinutes,
118	        usagePercent: UsagePercent,
119	        now: Date
120	    ) -> MenuBarSegment? {
121	        var parts: [String] = []
122	        if display.showLetter, !display.letter.isEmpty {
123	            parts.append(display.letter)
124	        }
125	        if display.showPercent {
126	            parts.append("\(usagePercent.rawValue)%")
127	        }
128	        if display.showReset {
129	            let remaining = resetAt.map { formatRemainingTime(resetAt: $0, now: now) } ?? "???"
130	            parts.append(remaining)
131	        }
132	        let text = parts.joined(separator: " ")
133	
134	        let tier: ConsumptionTier?
135	        if display.showDot {
136	            let theoretical = resetAt.map { theoreticalFraction(resetAt: $0, windowDuration: windowDuration, now: now) } ?? 0.0
137	            tier = consumptionRatio(actualPercent: usagePercent, theoreticalFraction: theoretical)
138	                .map { consumptionTier(ratio: $0) }
139	        } else {
140	            tier = nil
141	        }
142	
143	        // If the user turned every visible element off, there's nothing to render
144	        if text.isEmpty, !display.showDot, !display.showVendorIcon {
145	            return nil
146	        }
147	
148	        let vendorIcon: Vendor? = display.showVendorIcon ? vendor : nil
149	        return MenuBarSegment(text: text, tier: tier, showDot: display.showDot, vendorIcon: vendorIcon)
150	    }
151	
152	    private static func renderPayAsYouGo(currentAmount: Double, currency: String) -> MenuBarSegment {
153	        let amount = String(format: "%.2f", currentAmount)
154	        return MenuBarSegment(text: "\(amount) \(currency)", tier: nil, showDot: false)
155	    }
156	}

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

jobs:
  release:
    name: Build and Release
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Select Xcode 26
        # Swift 6.2 (Xcode 26) is required because the codebase uses stable
        # @MainActor deinit, which is experimental in Swift 6.1 (Xcode 16.x).
        run: |
          XCODE_PATH=$(ls -d /Applications/Xcode_26*.app 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1)
          if [ -z "$XCODE_PATH" ]; then
            echo "No Xcode 26.x found on runner. Available:"
            ls -d /Applications/Xcode*.app
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Using: $XCODE_PATH"
          sudo xcode-select -s "$XCODE_PATH/Contents/Developer"

      - name: Show Build Environment
        run: |
          xcodebuild -version
          swift --version
          echo "macOS: $(sw_vers -productVersion)"
          echo "Tag: ${{ github.ref_name }}"

      - name: Extract version from tag
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Version: ${VERSION}"

      - name: Strip SwiftLint plugin for CI build
        run: |
          # SwiftLintPlugins fails on CI runners with "prebuild command cannot
          # use executables built from source". Lint is a dev/PR concern, not
          # a release concern — strip the plugin so the release build proceeds.
          python3 -c "
          import re, pathlib
          p = pathlib.Path('AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift')
          s = p.read_text()
          s = re.sub(r',\s*plugins:\s*\[[^\]]*\]', '', s)
          p.write_text(s)
          "
          echo "--- Patched Package.swift ---"
          cat AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift

      - name: Build app bundle
        env:
          BUNDLE_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh
          echo "✅ Build completed"

      - name: Create release artifact
        id: artifact
        run: |
          cd dist
          ditto -c -k --keepParent "AI Usages Tracker.app" "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip"
          shasum -a 256 "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip" > "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"
          echo "SHA256:"
          cat "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          draft: false
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Compute SHA256 for Homebrew
        id: sha
        run: |
          SHA=$(shasum -a 256 dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip | awk '{print $1}')
          echo "sha256=${SHA}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "SHA256: ${SHA}"

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.ref_name }} published"
          echo ""
          echo "⚠️  Ad-hoc signed build (no Apple notarization)"
          echo "   First launch: right-click > Open to bypass Gatekeeper"

    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
      sha256: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.sha256 }}

  update-tap:
    name: Update Homebrew Tap
    needs: release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout homebrew-tap
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: fcamblor/homebrew-tap
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}

      - name: Update cask file
        run: |
          CASK="Casks/ai-usages-tracker.rb"
          [ -f "$CASK" ] || { echo "$CASK missing in tap"; exit 1; }

          sed -i "s/version \".*\"/version \"${{ needs.release.outputs.version }}\"/" "$CASK"
          sed -i "s/sha256 \".*\"/sha256 \"${{ needs.release.outputs.sha256 }}\"/" "$CASK"

          grep -q "version \"${{ needs.release.outputs.version }}\"" "$CASK"
          grep -q "sha256 \"${{ needs.release.outputs.sha256 }}\"" "$CASK"

          echo "--- Updated cask ---"
          cat "$CASK"

      - name: Commit and push
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Casks/ai-usages-tracker.rb
          git commit -m "chore: bump ai-usages-tracker to ${{ needs.release.outputs.version }}"
          git push

content.text:
L'explication tient à un détail simple : **les deux builds n'utilisent pas le même domaine de `UserDefaults`**, parce qu'ils n'ont pas le même bundle identifier.

## Ce qui se passe

`UserDefaults.standard` se base sur `Bundle.main.bundleIdentifier` pour choisir le fichier `.plist` :

- **Build Homebrew** : c'est un vrai `.app` bundle avec `Info.plist` qui définit `CFBundleIdentifier = io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker` (cf. `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh:15` et l'`Info.plist` généré). Donc `UserDefaults` écrit dans `~/Library/Preferences/io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker.plist`.
- **Build local via `swift run`** : SwiftPM produit juste un binaire dans `.build/...` sans `Info.plist`. `Bundle.main.bundleIdentifier` est `nil`, donc `UserDefaults.standard` retombe sur un domaine basé sur le nom de l'exécutable (typiquement `~/Library/Preferences/AIUsagesTrackers.plist`, ou un domaine en mémoire selon le contexte de lancement).

Ce sont donc **deux fichiers de préférences totalement disjoints**. Tes segments configurés en local ne sont pas visibles par la version Homebrew, c'est attendu.

## Pourquoi rien ne s'affiche côté Homebrew

Normalement, `MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.seedIfNeeded` (appelé dans `AppDelegate.swift:61`) doit semer 2 segments par défaut au premier lancement quand `menuBarSegmentsInitialized` est `false`. Donc en théorie, sur une install Homebrew toute neuve, tu devrais voir les 2 segments Claude (S et W).

Pistes les plus probables si tu n'as rien :

1. **Le `AppPidGuard` (`AppDelegate.swift:39-56`) tue l'app au démarrage** si une instance `swift run` tourne en parallèle → `applicationDidFinishLaunching` se termine avant `seedIfNeeded`. Vérifie qu'aucune autre instance ne tourne (`ps aux | grep AIUsagesTrackers`) et qu'il n'y a pas un PID resté dans `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/`.
2. **Le compte Claude n'est pas vu comme "active"** : les segments seedés visent `account: .currentlyActive`. Si l'app Homebrew n'arrive pas à lire `~/.claude/...` (Gatekeeper / quarantaine au premier launch via `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine`), `MenuBarSegmentResolver` renvoie `noActiveAccount` et `applyFormat` n'a rien à afficher → fallback text.
3. **L'app crashe silencieusement avant la fin du `applicationDidFinishLaunching`** (par exemple sur le code-sign ad-hoc). Regarde Console.app et `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/` pour les logs.

## Pour diagnostiquer

```bash
# Voir si le plist a bien été créé
defaults read io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker
# Forcer le réseed en cas de doute
defaults delete io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker menuBarSegmentsInitialized
```

Si `defaults read` retourne "domain does not exist", c'est que `applicationDidFinishLaunching` n'est pas allé jusqu'à `seedIfNeeded` — c'est probablement le pid guard ou un crash early. Les logs Console.app te donneront la cause exacte.

content.text:
Trouvé. Le crash est explicite :

```
AIUsagesTrackers/resource_bundle_accessor.swift:12: Fatal error: could not load resource bundle:
  from /Users/fcamblor/Applications/AI Usages Tracker.app/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle
  or  /Users/runner/work/.../release/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle
```

## Cause

Dans `AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift:25`, la cible exécutable déclare des ressources :

```swift
resources: [.copy("Resources/VendorBranding")],
```

Quand un target SwiftPM a des ressources, SwiftPM génère automatiquement un `resource_bundle_accessor.swift` (pour `Bundle.module`) qui cherche un bundle nommé `AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle`. Ce bundle est produit à côté du binaire dans `.build/.../release/`.

Le script `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh:46` ne copie **que le binaire** dans le `.app` :

```bash
cp "$APP_BIN" "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
```

…mais oublie de copier `AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle`. Résultat :

- **`swift run` localement** : le binaire trouve son bundle voisin → tout marche, le seeder s'exécute, les segments par défaut sont écrits, l'icône Claude se charge, etc.
- **App Homebrew** : le binaire ne trouve pas le bundle → `fatalError` au premier accès à `Bundle.module` (probablement `VendorBranding` lors du rendu du menu bar). L'app crashe avant que `seedIfNeeded` n'ait écrit quoi que ce soit dans le plist → le fichier `io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker.plist` n'existe pas.

## Fix

Modifier `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh` pour copier aussi le bundle de ressources SwiftPM. Quelque chose comme :

```bash
echo "→ Copying SwiftPM resource bundle"
RESOURCE_BUNDLE="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle"
if [ -d "$RESOURCE_BUNDLE" ]; then
  cp -R "$RESOURCE_BUNDLE" "$MACOS_DIR/"
fi
```

Le bundle doit être à côté du binaire (dans `Contents/MacOS/`), pas dans `Contents/Resources/`, parce que le `resource_bundle_accessor` généré le cherche via `Bundle.main.bundleURL.appendingPathComponent("AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle")` puis `Bundle.main.executableURL?.deletingLastPathComponent()` — le second chemin pointe sur `Contents/MacOS/`. Mettre les deux endroits si tu veux être défensif.

Après ce fix, il faudra rebuild + republier (et bumper la version Homebrew) pour que les nouvelles installations aient le seeder qui tourne et créent le plist avec les 2 segments par défaut.

Tu veux que j'applique le patch sur `build-app-bundle.sh` ?

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Build a distributable .app bundle for the menubar app.
# Output: dist/AI Usages Tracker.app (ad-hoc signed, unsealed).
#
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
PACKAGE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/AIUsagesTrackers"
DIST_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/dist"

APP_DISPLAY_NAME="AI Usages Tracker"
APP_BINARY_NAME="AIUsagesTrackers"
BUNDLE_ID="io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker"
BUNDLE_VERSION="${BUNDLE_VERSION:-0.1.0}"
MIN_MACOS_VERSION="14.0"

APP_BUNDLE="$DIST_DIR/$APP_DISPLAY_NAME.app"
CONTENTS="$APP_BUNDLE/Contents"
MACOS_DIR="$CONTENTS/MacOS"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS/Resources"
BUILD_CONFIG="release"
BUILD_DIR="$PACKAGE_DIR/.build/$BUILD_CONFIG"

echo "→ Building release binaries"
cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product "$APP_BINARY_NAME"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product IconExporter

# Resolve absolute build paths (swift emits a symlink in some configurations).
APP_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
ICON_EXPORTER_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/IconExporter"

echo "→ Assembling bundle at $APP_BUNDLE"
rm -rf "$APP_BUNDLE"
mkdir -p "$MACOS_DIR" "$RESOURCES_DIR"

echo "→ Rendering .icns from AppIconView"
ICONSET_TMP="$(mktemp -d)/AppIcon.iconset"
mkdir -p "$ICONSET_TMP"
"$ICON_EXPORTER_BIN" "$ICONSET_TMP"
iconutil -c icns "$ICONSET_TMP" -o "$RESOURCES_DIR/AppIcon.icns"

echo "→ Copying executable"
cp "$APP_BIN" "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
chmod +x "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"

echo "→ Writing Info.plist"
cat > "$CONTENTS/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
  <string>$APP_BINARY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>APPL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>
  <string>AppIcon</string>
  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
  <string>$MIN_MACOS_VERSION</string>
  <key>LSUIElement</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHumanReadableCopyright</key>
  <string>© $(date +%Y) Frédéric Camblor</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST

echo "→ Ad-hoc code-signing (required for Gatekeeper on unsigned binaries)"
codesign --force --deep --sign - "$APP_BUNDLE"

echo
echo "✓ Bundle ready: $APP_BUNDLE"
echo "  Launch with: open \"$APP_BUNDLE\""

echo "→ Copying SwiftPM resource bundles"
BIN_PATH="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)"
shopt -s nullglob
for bundle in "$BIN_PATH"/*.bundle; do
  bundle_name="$(basename "$bundle")"
  echo "  • $bundle_name"
  cp -R "$bundle" "$MACOS_DIR/"
  # SwiftPM emits a flat bundle (resources at the root, no Info.plist).
  # codesign rejects that as "bundle format unrecognized" — give it a minimal
  # Info.plist so the embedded bundle can be signed with the parent .app.
  bundle_basename="${bundle_name%.bundle}"
  cat > "$MACOS_DIR/$bundle_name/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID.resources.$bundle_basename</string>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$bundle_basename</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>BNDL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST
done
shopt -u nullglob

Build OK et signature validée — le bundle de ressources SwiftPM (`AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle`) est maintenant inclus dans `Contents/MacOS/`, avec un `Info.plist` minimal pour qu'il soit signable.

## Résumé du fix (`scripts/build-app-bundle.sh`)

1. Après la copie du binaire, parcours de `*.bundle` dans `swift build --show-bin-path` et copie dans `Contents/MacOS/` (à côté du binaire, là où `Bundle.module` les cherche).
2. Génération d'un `Info.plist` minimal (`CFBundlePackageType = BNDL`, identifiant dérivé du bundle id principal) à la racine de chaque bundle copié, sinon `codesign --deep` rejette le flat bundle.

Tu peux tester : `open "dist/AI Usages Tracker.app"` — l'icône doit apparaître dans la barre de menu, et au premier lancement le seeder devrait écrire les 2 segments par défaut dans `~/Library/Preferences/io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker.plist`.

Une fois validé, il faudra tag/release (`vX.Y.Z`) pour que le workflow GitHub republie une archive et mette à jour le cask Homebrew.

content.text:
# Smart commit

Tu dois committer les changements git en cours dans le repo courant en suivant les guidelines ci-dessous.

## Contexte utilisateur

Intent fourni par l'utilisateur (peut être vide) : ``

## Étapes

### 1. Analyser l'état du repo

Lance en parallèle :
- `git status` (sans `-uall`)
- `git diff --staged` et `git diff` pour voir les changements
- `git log -n 5 --pretty=format:'%h %s%n%b%n---'` pour voir les 4-5 derniers commits

### 2. Détecter les conventions du projet

À partir des 4-5 derniers commits :
- **Conventional commits ?** Si la majorité suit le format `type(scope): message` (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, build, ci...) → utilise ce format. Sinon → format libre.
- **Langue ?** Détecte la langue dominante des messages (français, anglais, autre) → utilise la même langue. En cas de doute, français.
- **Style ?** Impératif vs descriptif, majuscule initiale ou non, présence de scope, etc. → imite.

### 3. Détecter un éventuel fixup

Si l'intent utilisateur mentionne un correctif (mots-clés : "fix", "corrige", "répare", "bug", "issue", "problème", "régression", "broken"...) :

1. Identifie les fichiers modifiés (`git diff --name-only HEAD` + fichiers staged).
2. Pour chaque fichier, regarde `git log -n 10 --oneline -- <fichier>` pour voir quels commits récents l'ont touché.
3. Si **un seul commit récent** (dans les ~5 derniers, non encore pushé sur main/master partagé — vérifie avec `git log @{u}..HEAD` si upstream) est clairement à l'origine du code cassé, propose un **`git commit --fixup=<sha>`** au lieu d'un commit classique.
4. Si ambigu ou si le commit date d'avant `@{u}` (déjà pushé en amont partagé), reste sur un commit classique.

Ne fais **pas** de rebase autosquash automatiquement — laisse ce soin à l'utilisateur.

### 4. Rédiger le message

Règles impératives :
- **Le message explique le POURQUOI**, pas le QUOI. Le diff dit déjà ce qui change. Le message dit pourquoi ce changement existe (motivation, contrainte, problème résolu, contexte métier).
- Sujet court (≤72 caractères), en suivant la convention détectée.
- Si le pourquoi ne tient pas dans le sujet, ajoute un corps (ligne vide + explication). Sinon, pas de corps inutile.
- Pas de name-dropping de fichiers/fonctions dans le sujet — le diff suffit.
- Pas de phrases vides genre « Mise à jour du code » ou « Divers fixes ».

Si l'intent utilisateur est vide ou trop vague pour déduire le pourquoi, et que le diff ne le rend pas évident, **demande à l'utilisateur** avant de committer plutôt que d'inventer.

### 5. Staging et commit

- Si rien n'est staged, stage les fichiers pertinents **par nom** (jamais `git add -A` ni `git add .` pour éviter d'embarquer secrets/binaires).
- Ignore les fichiers suspects (`.env`, `credentials*`, clés privées, dumps volumineux). Préviens l'utilisateur si tu en vois.
- Crée le commit via HEREDOC pour préserver le formatage multiligne.
- **Ne jamais** utiliser `--no-verify`, `--amend` (sauf demande explicite), ni `--no-gpg-sign`.
- Si un hook pre-commit échoue : diagnostique la cause, corrige, re-stage, et crée un **nouveau** commit (pas d'amend).

### 6. Vérification finale

Après commit, lance `git status` et `git log -1` pour confirmer. Reporte brièvement : sha court + sujet + (fixup le cas échéant).

## Garde-fous

- Ne push jamais.
- Ne crée pas de commit vide.
- Ne touche pas à `git config`.
- Si plusieurs "unités logiques" distinctes sont mélangées dans le diff, préviens l'utilisateur et propose de splitter plutôt que de tout entasser. Tu peux utiliser `git stash` (avec `-u`/`--keep-index` si pertinent) pour isoler des sous-ensembles — mais cette commande n'est **pas** auto-approuvée, l'utilisateur devra confirmer à chaque usage : ne l'utilise que si c'est vraiment nécessaire.

Remove four completed epics from the roadmap: claude-status-connector,

These features have been shipped and are no longer planned work.
Update index.md to reflect the simplified roadmap with only the

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: d5df9348af2a

---
0350127 feat: add usage history charts to popover

---
d8322f5 fix(snapshots): partition daily files by UTC date to match line timestamps
The daily file name was derived from Calendar.current (local timezone), so
timestamps near midnight could end up in the wrong file — e.g. a line with
timestamp 2026-04-25T00:30:00Z landing in 2026-04-24.jsonl on UTC+1 hosts.

SnapshotRecorder now defaults to a UTC gregorian calendar so the file-name
date is always identical to the date visible in the UTC timestamps embedded
in each line. The calendar parameter remains injectable so tests can still
override it; passing nil (the new default) selects UTC.

Adds a test that verifies a timestamp just after UTC midnight routes to the
correct UTC-day file regardless of the host's local timezone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: a196732e4f60

---
b313c4a feat(snapshots): add periodic usage history recording with content-based deduplication
Implements TickSnapshot model with AccountSnapshot and MetricSnapshot types
for serializing one JSONL line per tick containing every vendor/account/metric
captured at that instant. SnapshotRecorder actor writes to
~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl
on a fixed 60-second cadence (SnapshotScheduler), independent of the user's
configurable polling interval.

Deduplication compares the SHA256 hash of the payload (excluding timestamp) to
the last-written snapshot, skipping duplicate ticks and avoiding bloat during
idle periods. The hash persists across process restarts: on the first tick
after init, SnapshotRecorder reads the last line of today's daily file to seed
its in-memory hash state, ensuring the first post-crash tick doesn't append a
duplicate of the last-written state.

Each JSONL line is crafted so timestamp field appears first (for readability);
accounts array is filtered to omit those with only unknown metrics. Calendar
is injectable so tests can run deterministically in UTC.

18 new tests cover nominal operation, skips (empty, unchanged, unknown metrics),
file creation on first tick, append across recorder restart (the critical
dedup-across-restart case), day rollover, JSON validity, and new-day startup.
All 361 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: bba09c4dfc26

---

input.command:
git add scripts/build-app-bundle.sh && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(build-app-bundle): include SwiftPM resource bundles in app bundle

SwiftPM emits resource bundles (AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle)
next to the executable when targets declare `resources:`. The build script
was not copying these into the .app bundle, causing Bundle.module to
fatal-error on first access (crash before applicationDidFinishLaunching
completed, so seeding never ran).

Now copies all *.bundle files from the build directory alongside the binary
in Contents/MacOS/, where Bundle.module looks for them. Also generates a
minimal Info.plist in each bundle so codesign --deep accepts the flat bundle
format and can sign the parent app without rejecting embedded bundles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log -1
